steps.title: Identify Plot's reputation
steps.content: Plot's reputation was that he was the first person to discover dinosaur remains. The 1990 discovery matters because it suggests Indigenous peoples may have found such remains centuries earlier.

steps.title: Reject misstatements of significance
steps.content:
• Choice A: It wrongly narrows Plot's reputation to discovery of M. carinatus remains.
  • Choice C: It describes the discovery but does not connect it to Plot's reputation.
  • Choice D: It overstates the evidence as disproving Plot's own claims.

steps.title: Connect the discovery to reputation
steps.content: The best sentence explains that evidence of an earlier Indigenous find suggests Plot may not have been the first person to discover dinosaur remains. The answer is B.
